title: HUF Collective Distribution Report — April 7, 2026: The Wave Mechanics Session
distribution: All collective participants (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Copilot, Peter)
supersedes: None. Extends CRPT-009 (April 5 PM: Conference Alignment and Validation Roadmap).
purpose: Session S016 began with a timezone self-correction on Fukushima data and cascaded into four quantitative findings (spectral independence, carrier impulse response, dependency chain, two-phase CR model), three governance documents (charter, proof burden register, battle card), a corpus revision brief proposing 27-to-10 document consolidation, and the reframing of HUF as a compositional disassembler derived from wave mechanics.

items_resolved_from_crpt_009_open_items.was: Planned as 3x3 Spearman/Pearson correlation matrix. HIGHEST PRIORITY.
items_resolved_from_crpt_009_open_items.now: FFT power spectrum analysis shows three diagnostics concentrate power in different frequency bands: CR at 2-3yr (fast coupling), Aitchison at 4-12yr (ratio restructuring), TV at 3-25yr (secular trends). Mean |r| = 0.231 (time domain), 0.286 (frequency domain). Result is STRONGER than planned — shows WHY diagnostics are independent, not just that they have low correlation.

items_resolved_from_crpt_009_open_items.was: Simulation study planned for Week 2. CR has no theoretical basis.
items_resolved_from_crpt_009_open_items.now: Two-phase CR model: CR correlates with group delay mismatch at impulse (r=+0.61, Fukushima 2010-2011) but NOT at settling (r=-0.15, 2011-2012). Phase 1 = distributed system response dynamics. Phase 2 = structural reorganisation. SBP reframed as filter bank (mathematical equivalence, not analogy). Still needs simulation validation.
'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator' with 'Three-Diagnostic Protocol (TV + Aitchison + CR)'. 5 occurrences replaced.

items_still_open_from_crpt_009.approach: Sensitivity sweep: vary each threshold, count patterns, check if 31% STRUCTURAL is robust.
Dispatchable vs Intermittent at root. Rerun CR. Compare patterns. Note: S016 SBP phase alignment analysis may inform the alternative design.
Generate compositions with known coupling structure, compute CR, verify tracking. Two-phase model provides testable predictions for simulation design.

finding_1_spectral_independence.title: Three diagnostics operate in different frequency bands
finding_1_spectral_independence.method: FFT power spectrum of TV, Aitchison, CR annual time series for Germany, Japan, UK
finding_1_spectral_independence.key_result: CR concentrates power at 2-3yr periods (fast coupling). Aitchison at 4-12yr (ratio restructuring). TV at 3-25yr (secular composition trends). Mean absolute time-domain correlation: 0.231. Frequency-domain: 0.286.
finding_1_spectral_independence.significance: Addresses W-1. The diagnostics are not redundant — they observe the same system at different temporal scales. The n=3 claim survives.
finding_1_spectral_independence.named_principle: Scarborough Bluffs Principle — confound-to-signal ratio is a function of measurement resolution, not a fixed property.

finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.title: Carrier group delays reveal SBP as filter bank
finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.method: Fukushima event used as natural impulse. Fractional deviation from 2008-2010 baseline measured per carrier. Group delay = time to peak response.
finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.key_result: Carrier group delays: Other_Fossil 1yr, Nuclear 3yr, Coal 4yr, Gas 5yr, Solar 7yr+. SBP phase alignment check: 3 of 5 balances misaligned (Gas vs Other_Fossil: delta 4yr). Two aligned (Solar vs Wind: delta 0yr). The misalignment IS the governance signal.
finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.two_phase_cr_model: CR correlates with group delay mismatch at impulse (r=+0.61, 2010→2011) but not at settling (r=-0.15, 2011→2012). Phase 1: distributed dynamics. Phase 2: structural reorganisation.
finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.loudspeaker_bridge: SBP is isomorphic to a multi-channel filter bank. Each ILR balance is a band-pass channel. Group delays in energy carriers correspond to driver group delays in loudspeakers. The crossover network IS the Sequential Binary Partition.
finding_2_carrier_impulse_response.significance: Partially addresses W-2 (CR grounding). Provides testable predictions for simulation study. Reframes HUF origin story from biographical to methodological.

finding_3_dependency_chain.title: Closure forces a relay chain through carriers
finding_3_dependency_chain.method: Year-over-year share changes for Japan carriers. Cross-correlation matrix. Handoff detection (role transitions). CR by cascade stage.
finding_3_dependency_chain.key_result: Five-stage chain: SHOCK (Nuclear -24pp) → EMERGENCY (Other_Fossil +12pp) → HANDOFF_1 (Gas absorbs) → HANDOFF_2 (Coal absorbs) → LONG_TAIL (Solar slowly rises). Cross-correlation confirms relay: Nuclear↔Gas r=-0.74. Key finding: CR is NOT elevated during handoffs (mean 0.566 vs 0.660 at non-handoff). Handoffs are decoupling moments.
finding_3_dependency_chain.loudspeaker_bridge: In a loudspeaker, drivers are independent (each has its own amplifier). In a composition, carriers are relay partners through closure (sum = 1). This is the fundamental difference between loudspeaker physics and compositional physics.
finding_3_dependency_chain.significance: Explains why CR drops during handoffs — the system is temporarily less coupled. Supports the disassembler interpretation: HUF reveals the internal relay structure of compositions.

finding_4_reframing.title: HUF is a compositional disassembler derived from wave mechanics
finding_4_reframing.description: Full corpus scrape confirmed the wave-mechanics interpretation was already latent across WHAT_HUF_IS.md ('The crossover network is a Sequential Binary Partition. The drivers are carriers.'), THE_LINEAGE.md (DADC/DADI/ADAC), THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC.md (coupling leakage across SBP branches), and RogueWaveAudio build scripts (group delay equation, DADC decomposition). S016 made this empirical rather than biographical.
finding_4_reframing.alignment_sentence_2_updated: Three diagnostics — TV, Aitchison, CR — operating in different frequency bands of structural change.
finding_4_reframing.significance: The loudspeaker origin is no longer only a story. It is a methodological derivation. The SBP IS a filter bank. The group delays ARE measurable. The crossover coherence IS the coherence residual.

charter.description: Nine-article formal governance charter. Article II: Governed Breakpoint Principle with E-Stop refinement (de-energize correction output while observation path stays live). Article III: Right to Interrupt Principle. Article IV: Open-loop priority. Article V: Five integrity commitments. Article VIII: Six preserved rights. Article IX: Declaration.
charter.e_stop_refinement: The governed breakpoint does not stop governance. It de-energizes the correction output while the observation path remains live. Like an emergency stop on industrial machinery — the sensors stay active, the control logic keeps running, but power is removed from the actuators.
charter.origin: Developed from Peter/ChatGPT conversation. Peter's key insight: 'the E-Stop on machines does the interrupt by removing power from the active components in a controlled loop. The governance is not stopped; the ability to perform any action under power is.'

proof_burden_register.description: Nine items covering every major claim HUF makes for Coimbra. Each maps: current claim → proof burden → most likely misunderstanding → safe wording → answer discipline. Rewrite filter: keep a sentence only if it reduces misunderstanding more than it increases proof burden.
proof_burden_register.origin: Co-developed by ChatGPT and Peter. Claude formatted and filed.

battle_card.description: Two-column live rehearsal card. 15 pressure-point questions with calibrated live answers. Safe/red-flag phrase lists. Three anchor sentences. Three named principles table (Scarborough Bluffs, Governed Breakpoint, Right to Interrupt).
battle_card.origin: Co-developed by ChatGPT and Peter. Claude formatted and filed.

corpus_revision_brief.description: Comprehensive brief for ChatGPT-guided corpus consolidation. Proposes reducing 27 core codawork-2026 documents to ~10 coherent documents.

tier_1_conference_facing: THE_INSTRUMENT.md (merge of WHAT_HUF_IS + THE_LINEAGE + THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC + THE_UNION)
COHERENCE_RESIDUAL_RESULTS.md (expand with spectral independence and impulse response)

corpus_revision_brief.significance: First coherent plan to reduce document noise. ChatGPT to guide rewrite with cooperation lexicon (three-level vocabulary ladder: native CoDa, bridge terms, HUF-specific).

three_named_principles.description: S016 formalised three named principles. All now appear in the governance charter and battle card.

principles.name: Scarborough Bluffs Principle
principles.statement: Confound-to-signal ratio is a function of measurement resolution, not a fixed property.
principles.origin: Peter's geomorphology training — erosion rates depend on whether you measure hourly, yearly, or geologically.
principles.use: When challenged on temporal resolution, scale, or confounds.

principles.name: Governed Breakpoint Principle
principles.statement: A governed system shall preserve an observable breakpoint at the moment of self-correction.
principles.refinement: The breakpoint de-energizes correction output while the observation path stays live (E-Stop analogy).
principles.use: When challenged on automation, control, or safety.

principles.name: Right to Interrupt Principle
principles.statement: Any governed system must preserve the right to interrupt, modify, defer, or reject closure.
principles.bridge: The Governed Breakpoint Principle creates the Right to Interrupt.
principles.use: When challenged on governance or human oversight.

documents_modified_this_session.change: Title: 'Unified Command Dashboard' → 'CoDa Calibration Demonstrator'. Subtitle: 'Dual Metric Engine' → 'Three-Diagnostic Protocol (TV + Aitchison + CR)'. 5 occurrences.
S016 entry added. Pending items rewritten (W-1 addressed, analyzer done, new items for consolidation, charter, lexicon). File count updated to 316.

corpus_state.note: Analysis outputs (spectral, impulse, dependency chain JSONs/PNGs, revision brief) are in Claude CoWorker folder. Decision pending: which analysis outputs should be promoted into the repo (likely the JSONs into data/codawork-samples/).

governance_state.note: W-1 (spectral independence) addressed but governance state does not advance until W-3 (thresholds) and W-4 (SBP sensitivity) are completed. The n=3 claim is now empirically supported by frequency-domain evidence, not just cross-domain replication.

collective_conversation_topics.description: S016 has changed enough that the collective needs to reconvene. The following topics require collective input before the corpus consolidation proceeds.

topics.topic: Wave mechanics reframing: is it real or is it waffle?
topics.question: S016 showed SBP = filter bank, group delays are measurable, crossover coherence = CR. Is this a genuine methodological derivation or a compelling but unfounded analogy? What would it take to formalise the mapping?
topics.risk_if_wrong: If the mapping is analogy not equivalence, the loudspeaker bridge must be downgraded from 'methodological derivation' to 'inspirational source domain' in all documents.

topics.question: The revision brief proposes merging WHAT_HUF_IS + THE_LINEAGE + THE_THIRD_DIAGNOSTIC + THE_UNION into THE_INSTRUMENT.md. Is this the right merge? What gets lost? What gets clarified?
topics.risk_if_wrong: Bad merge creates incoherence worse than 27 separate documents.

topics.question: S016 proposes updating alignment sentence 2 from 'The instrument uses three diagnostics: total variation distance, Aitchison distance, and the coherence residual' to 'Three diagnostics — TV, Aitchison, CR — operating in different frequency bands of structural change.' Is the new version better for Coimbra? Does 'frequency bands' invite proof burden?
topics.who_should_lead: All — this affects every conference-facing artifact
topics.risk_if_wrong: 'Frequency bands' might imply signal processing claims the audience will challenge.

topics.question: The charter is at repo root. Should it stay there or move to huf-gov/governance/? How should it be cross-referenced in conference documents? Is it Front Room or Second Room for Coimbra?
topics.risk_if_wrong: Charter either distracts from science at Coimbra or gets buried where reviewers never find it.

topics.topic: Proof burden register: are the 9 items complete?
topics.question: The register covers MC-4, three diagnostics, CR, null model, zeros, loudspeaker bridge, governance, Ramsar, documentation volume. After S016's wave mechanics findings, should there be a 10th item for the filter-bank/group-delay claims?
topics.risk_if_wrong: Unregistered proof burden creates blind spots at Coimbra.

topics.question: S016 produced 3 JSON result files and 3 PNG visualisations in Claude CoWorker. Should some or all be promoted into data/codawork-samples/? The spectral independence JSON is direct evidence for W-1.
topics.risk_if_wrong: Evidence lives outside the repo where reviewers can't find it.

topics.question: The impulse response analysis suggests CR behaves differently at impulse vs settling. How should the W-2 simulation study be designed to test this? What coupling structures should be generated? What prediction does the two-phase model make that a null model does not?
topics.risk_if_wrong: Simulation study doesn't test the right thing.

topics.question: ChatGPT proposed a three-level vocabulary: (1) native CoDa terms, (2) bridge terms both communities understand, (3) HUF-specific terms that need definition. This needs to be built before the corpus rewrite. Who builds it? What format?

collective_review_instructions.for_all_reviewers: S016 is a pivot session. The project now has empirical evidence for spectral independence, a wave-mechanics methodological derivation, governance formalised in a charter, and a plan to consolidate the corpus. Review the 8 collective conversation topics above and provide your input before consolidation begins.
what_to_review: CCT-01 through CCT-08 — each needs collective input
HUF_GOVERNANCE_CHARTER.md — is this the right framing?
BATTLE_CARD_LIVE.md — are the 15 pressure-point answers calibrated correctly?
what_not_to_review: CRPT-008 and CRPT-009 findings — already integrated
Spectral analysis methodology — the FFT code is standard; review the interpretation, not the computation
Historical documents in archive/ — preserved, not for review
collective_review_instructions.peter_direction: A lot has changed. The collective needs to weigh in on the wave mechanics reframing and the consolidation plan before we rewrite. Send this report with the revision brief to ChatGPT first.

next_session_priorities.item: Collective conversation on 8 topics (CCT-01 through CCT-08)
next_session_priorities.blocks: Corpus consolidation cannot begin without collective alignment on reframing and architecture
SBP-dependence claim. S016 phase alignment analysis informs alternative design.

grok.summary: Full review of CRPT-010, governance charter, analyzer update, collective conversation document, and GitHub codawork-2026 folder. Confirmed all S016 outputs as coherent. Provided detailed SBP filter-bank formalization (channel frequencies, bandwidths). Proposed 5-analysis plan for maximizing existing data extraction. Extended pipeline conceptually to financial markets, Okavango, Amazon.

cct_inputs.CCT-01: Real, not waffle. Mathematical equivalence confirmed. HIGH confidence.
cct_inputs.CCT-05: Implicitly supports 10th proof burden item via own cross-domain extensions.
cct_inputs.CCT-07: Proposes global comparison as validation pathway. MEDIUM confidence.

flags_from_claude: Cross-domain claims asserted but not computed — financial/ecological group delays are extrapolations, not observations
10th proof burden item (PB-10: Cross-Domain Analogical Transfer) added to register in response
Each new domain needs its own SBP design — energy SBP cannot be borrowed
5-analysis plan sound as proposal but every step needs computation before claim

copilot.summary: Full architectural review. Confirmed direction is correct and system is aligned for the first time. Identified three structural risks: (1) wave-mechanics language too hot, (2) Tier-1 merge too aggressive, (3) S016 evidence not in repo. Provided CCT-by-CCT responses, revised table of contents (12 docs not 10), 4-phase rewrite plan, draft cooperation lexicon, and Coimbra-ready alignment sentence.

cct_inputs.CCT-01: Real but soften verbs. Move bridge to second room.
cct_inputs.CCT-02: Split into THE_INSTRUMENT.md + THE_LINEAGE_AND_BRIDGE.md. 4 Tier-1 docs not 3.
cct_inputs.CCT-03: New sentence: 'Three diagnostics — TV, Aitchison, and CR — each sensitive to different temporal patterns of structural change in the present sample.' Low proof burden.
cct_inputs.CCT-04: Keep charter at repo root. Second room only for Coimbra.
cct_inputs.CCT-05: PB-10 confirmed necessary. Ensure it appears in Tier-2 docs.
cct_inputs.CCT-06: Promote all S016 JSONs to data/codawork-samples/S016/.
cct_inputs.CCT-07: Write explicit falsifiable predictions before simulation. >= 500 runs per condition.

gemini.summary: Strongest endorsement of any reviewer. Called S016 'the most significant leap in the project's history.' Verdict: GO FOR COIMBRA. Coined 'Methodological Isomorphism' as middle ground between formal algebraic isomorphism and mere analogy. Proposed three specific tests for Claude to solidify claim. Validated self-correction integrity, battle card discipline, and Scarborough Bluffs Principle. Proposed 'Scientific Surrender' posture with three examination requests for Coimbra.

cct_inputs.CCT-01: Real — 'Methodological Isomorphism.' Strongest endorsement but conditional on three tests. Audio as explanation, CoDa as proof.
cct_inputs.CCT-05: Supports proof burden discipline. Warns against hot language obscuring Aitchison geometry.
cct_inputs.CCT-07: Three specific tests: (1) Backblaze group delay core vs diversity tail, (2) Spectral independence on Backblaze, (3) Impulse relay formalization on Fukushima.
cct_inputs.CCT-08: Audio terms as explanation, CoDa terms as proof. Clear lexicon guidance.

proposed_tests_for_claude: Spectral independence (W-1) on Backblaze time series
Group delay: Backblaze core fleet vs diversity tail during replacement events
Impulse relay mapping formalization on Fukushima

coimbra_strategy.posture: Scientific Surrender — request for peer review, not claim of breakthrough
examination_requests: Calibration: Is the COVID structural lead a valid finding or Aitchison geometry artifact?
Joint analysis: 17 error sources at the HUF-CoDa joint — request calibration expertise
Missing Quarter: Can CoDa control charts detect Deceptive Drift without HUF monitoring?

one_sentence: S016 began with a Fukushima timezone self-correction and cascaded into four quantitative findings (spectral independence addressing W-1, carrier impulse response with two-phase CR model partially addressing W-2, dependency chain revealing closure-forced relay, and SBP-as-filter-bank reframing HUF as a compositional disassembler derived from wave mechanics), three governance documents (9-article charter with E-Stop refinement, 9-item proof burden register, 15-question battle card), a corpus revision brief proposing 27-to-10 consolidation, and 8 collective conversation topics requiring collective input before the rewrite begins.
